publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Files consist of research materials from his books and other reporting.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Daniel S. Greenberg (May 9, 1931 – March 9, 2020), science journalist and author, graduated from Columbia University in 1953. In 1961, he became the first editor of the "News and Comment " section of the journal Science. He also worked as a science reporter for the Washington Post. In 1971 he founded the Science and Government Report, a fortnightly review of government grants, reports, and activities in science. He served as its editor until 1997, when it was acquired by John Wiley & Sons. He also authored several books on the politics of science in the United States, including The Politics of Pure Science (1967), Science, Money and Politics (2001), Science for Sale (2007), and Tech Transfer (2010).

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Files consist of research materials from his books and other reporting, including media clips, Congressional reports, transcripts, and agency reports, filed alphabetically by topic (with various subtopics, changes over time, etc.) Files also contain some interview notes, partial transcripts of interviews, and correspondence.

emph.ref: Series I: Miscellaneous Pamphlets, Clippings, Correspondence and Documents
Series II: News and Journal Clippings
Series III: University Information for "Science for Sale"
Series IV: Science and Technology Statistics
Series VI: Office of Technology Assessment
Series VII: Science and Government Report

list.head: Books - cataloged individually in the Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
list.item: Science and Government Report International Almanac, Daniel Greenberg, editor. 1977
Science and Government Report International Almanac, Daniel Greenberg, editor. 1978-1979

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Daniel S. Greenberg papers, #6993. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
